questions.q: What does Aadhaar primarily provide to a resident of India?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar gives each enrolled resident a unique 12-digit identification number linked to their demographic and biometric data.

questions.q: Which authority issues Aadhaar numbers in India?
questions.explanation: The Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) is the statutory body that issues Aadhaar numbers.

questions.q: Aadhaar is often described as the world's largest system of what kind?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is the largest biometric digital identity programme in the world, covering over a billion residents.

questions.q: How many digits does an Aadhaar number contain?
questions.explanation: An Aadhaar number is a 12-digit random number assigned to each resident.

questions.q: Which of the following biometrics is captured during Aadhaar enrolment?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar enrolment records ten fingerprints, both iris scans, and a facial photograph.

questions.q: Aadhaar is a programme run by which country?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is India's national biometric identity programme.

questions.q: Who is generally eligible to enrol for an Aadhaar number?
options: Only people above 60 years of age
questions.explanation: Aadhaar is available to any resident of India, and is a proof of identity, not of citizenship.

questions.q: What does the word 'Aadhaar' literally mean?
questions.explanation: 'Aadhaar' is a Hindi word meaning foundation or base, reflecting its role as a foundational identity.

questions.q: Roughly how many people are enrolled in Aadhaar, making it the largest of its kind?
questions.explanation: More than a billion residents have been enrolled in Aadhaar, making it the world's largest biometric ID system.

questions.q: Aadhaar can be used by residents primarily as a proof of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar serves as a widely accepted proof of identity and address across India.

questions.q: In which year was the UIDAI established to oversee the Aadhaar project?
questions.explanation: The UIDAI was established in January 2009 under the Planning Commission to implement the Aadhaar project.

questions.q: Who was the founding Chairman of the UIDAI when it began the Aadhaar project?
questions.explanation: Nandan Nilekani, co-founder of Infosys, served as the first Chairman of the UIDAI.

questions.q: When was the very first Aadhaar number issued?
questions.explanation: The first Aadhaar number was issued in September 2010 to a resident of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which law gave Aadhaar its statutory backing?
questions.explanation: The Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act was passed in 2016.

questions.q: The Aadhaar Act, 2016 was notably passed in Parliament as which type of bill?
questions.explanation: The Aadhaar Act, 2016 was introduced and passed as a money bill, a move that was later challenged in court.

questions.q: Aadhaar enables KYC and benefit transfers chiefly through what kind of verification service?
questions.explanation: UIDAI provides online authentication that verifies a person's biometric or demographic details against the Aadhaar database.

questions.q: Aadhaar is widely linked to which payments innovation for direct benefit transfers?
questions.explanation: Aadhaar underpins Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), routing subsidies straight into Aadhaar-linked bank accounts.

questions.q: Under which government ministry does the UIDAI currently function?
options: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
questions.explanation: The UIDAI now operates under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).

questions.q: Which of these is a paperless offline form of Aadhaar that protects privacy?
questions.explanation: UIDAI offers masked Aadhaar and a Virtual ID (VID) so users can authenticate without revealing the full Aadhaar number.

questions.q: The JAM trinity, a key plank of Indian welfare delivery, combines Aadhaar with which two others?
options: Jan Dhan accounts and Mobile numbers
questions.explanation: JAM stands for Jan Dhan bank accounts, Aadhaar, and Mobile numbers, used together for direct benefit transfers.

questions.q: Aadhaar data is stored and matched at UIDAI's central system, often referred to as the:
questions.explanation: The Central Identities Data Repository (CIDR) is UIDAI's centralised database that stores Aadhaar records and performs authentication.

questions.q: Which document does UIDAI issue to a resident after successful enrolment?
options: An Aadhaar letter or card carrying the number
questions.explanation: After enrolment, UIDAI sends an Aadhaar letter (and offers e-Aadhaar) bearing the resident's Aadhaar number.

questions.q: In the 2018 Puttaswamy (Aadhaar) judgment, the Supreme Court of India broadly upheld Aadhaar but struck down which provision?
options: Mandatory Aadhaar linking by private companies under Section 57
questions.explanation: The Court upheld Aadhaar's constitutionality but read down Section 57, barring private entities from compelling Aadhaar authentication.

questions.q: The 2017 Supreme Court ruling that strongly shaped Aadhaar's limits declared which fundamental right?
options: Right to privacy as a fundamental right
questions.explanation: In K. S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017), a nine-judge bench held that the right to privacy is a fundamental right.

questions.q: Before the Aadhaar Act of 2016, the UIDAI initially operated under which body?
questions.explanation: The UIDAI was first set up in 2009 as an attached office under the Planning Commission of India.

questions.q: Aadhaar's identification approach relies on de-duplication to ensure uniqueness. What technology makes this possible at scale?
options: Biometric de-duplication matching every new record against all existing ones
questions.explanation: UIDAI runs large-scale biometric de-duplication, comparing each enrolment's fingerprints and iris against the whole database to prevent duplicates.

questions.q: Which earlier scheme did Aadhaar's design draw upon or seek to complement for resident identification?
options: The Goods and Services Tax Network
questions.explanation: Aadhaar's identity effort ran alongside the National Population Register, both aiming to build a resident database, though they remained distinct.

questions.q: Aadhaar authentication for banking and SIM cards commonly uses which standardised mechanism?
options: e-KYC via biometric or OTP-based verification
questions.explanation: Aadhaar e-KYC lets service providers verify identity instantly using fingerprint, iris, or a one-time password sent to the registered mobile.

questions.q: What is the Virtual ID (VID) introduced by UIDAI designed to do?
options: Act as a revocable temporary number used in place of the Aadhaar number for authentication
Serve as a permanent passport number
questions.explanation: The Virtual ID is a temporary, revocable 16-digit number a user can generate to authenticate without sharing the actual Aadhaar number.

questions.q: A landmark consequence of Aadhaar-enabled DBT in subsidy delivery was widely cited as:
options: Reduction of leakages by removing duplicate and ghost beneficiaries
questions.explanation: By verifying unique identities, Aadhaar-linked Direct Benefit Transfer helped weed out duplicate and ghost beneficiaries, reducing subsidy leakages.
